subject: Sociology
title: Nationalism in a Global Age
description: A 60-second lesson on Nationalism in a Global Age.

hook.text: Globalization was supposed to make nations obsolete. Instead, nationalism surged worldwide. Nobody predicted that.

buildup.text: Nationalism is loyalty to one's nation and the belief that national interests should come first.

discovery.text: Globalization's disruptions — job losses, cultural change, inequality — fuel nationalist backlash and populist leaders.

twist.text: Nationalist leaders use global media to spread their anti-global message. The tools of globalization serve its critics.

climax.text: Nationalism isn't globalization's opposite. It's a reaction to it — born from the anxieties that connection creates.

quiz.question: Why has nationalism surged alongside globalization?
